result:
EXPLAIN shows query plan — how DB will execute SQL without running it.

Reveals:
- access method per table (seq scan, index scan, index-only scan)
- join order and join algorithm (nested loop, hash, merge)
- estimated row counts and cost per node
- filter and sort steps, whether sort spills to disk
- index usage — or missed indexes when you expected one

EXPLAIN ANALYZE actually runs query, adds real timings and actual row counts next to estimates. Gap between estimated and actual rows = stale stats or bad selectivity guess, common root cause of slow plans.

Read bottom-up: leaf nodes are table accesses, parents combine them.

Postgres adds BUFFERS (cache hits/misses), MySQL has EXPLAIN FORMAT=JSON / ANALYZE, SQLite has EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN (simpler tree).

Main uses: confirm index is hit, spot full scans on big tables, catch row-count blowups in joins, decide whether to rewrite query or add index.
